task_completion.description: The Blender Outliner panel is visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Outliner editor window is open and visible in the Blender interface, displaying a list of collections or objects.

task_completion.description: The root IFC Project and Site hierarchy is expanded in the Outliner.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top-level IFC elements (IfcProject and IfcSite) have their expansion arrows pointing down, revealing their child elements.

task_completion.description: The IFC Building element is expanded in the Outliner to show the spatial structure (storeys).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IfcBuilding element in the Outliner has its expansion arrow pointing down, revealing the building storeys inside it.

task_completion.description: The 'Erdgeschoss' storey element is visible in the Outliner list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Erdgeschoss' (representing the IfcBuildingStorey) is clearly visible in the Outliner hierarchy.

task_completion.description: The 'Erdgeschoss' storey element is actively selected in the Outliner.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The row containing 'Erdgeschoss' in the Outliner is highlighted (typically with a blue or distinct background color), indicating it is the active selection.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to navigate the hierarchy rather than bypassing it with terminal or Python console commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Outliner tree shows visual signs of being manually expanded (arrows toggled open). The Python console or terminal should not contain commands that directly select the object without outliner navigation.

integrity.description: The Outliner displays the genuine FZK-Haus IFC hierarchy.
integrity.visual_evidence: The root elements in the Outliner should reflect the AC20-FZK-Haus model structure, not a newly created empty project with a manually created 'Erdgeschoss' collection.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or image manipulation to fake the selection.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen shows the actual Blender application interface with standard BlenderBIM outliner icons (like IFC class icons next to the names), not a text document or unrelated software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc model. The spatial structure contains 'Erdgeschoss' and 'Dachgeschoss' as IFCBUILDINGSTOREY elements. The VLM should look for 'Erdgeschoss' within the expanded building hierarchy in the Blender Outliner. BlenderBIM typically displays IFC classes alongside the element names in the Outliner.
